summary: Iter17 Email/Password auth fallback — full backend + E2E verification. Backend: re-ran focused pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter16_email_auth.py -> 17/17 passed in 13s (registration unverified, verify-email logs in, login blocked until verified, brute-force lockout after 5 fails, forgot-password silent for unknown user, reset-password updates hash + auto-verifies + old password invalidated, single-use tokens). Frontend E2E executed against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with two fresh emails (e2e.iter17.<ts>@example.com and e2e.iter17.unver.<ts>@example.com): (1) Landing shows yellow 'Iniciar con email' + 'Comenzar con Google' CTAs; (2) dialog data-testid='email-auth-dialog' opens; (3) Google btn visible inside dialog (auth-google-btn) AND on landing (hero-start-btn); (4) auth-toggle-mode swaps to register; submit shows auth-success-state with 'Cuenta creada. Revisa tu correo...' and NO session_token cookie is set (i.e. no auto-login); (5) auth-forgot-link only present in login mode, switches title to 'Recuperar contraseña' and hides the password field; forgot submit shows neutral success message regardless of email existence; (6) Visiting /auth/verify-email/<token> renders verify-email-page, auto-creates session (httpOnly session_token cookie set) and redirects to /dashboard for role=normal; (7) Unverified login attempt returns success-state with 'Verifica tu correo antes de iniciar sesión. Revisa tu bandeja.' (not a toast) and NO cookie is set — matches spec; (8) /auth/reset-password/<token> renders, new pwd accepted via reset-pwd-input + reset-confirm-input + reset-submit, redirects to /; subsequent login with the new password via the dialog succeeds and redirects to /dashboard with a session cookie; (9) Toggle regression: login↔register cycles cleanly via auth-toggle-mode and never accidentally enters forgot mode. One minor UX glitch observed (see frontend_issues): VerifyEmail page briefly flashes 'Token inválido o ya usado' before the success+redirect — almost certainly React 18 StrictMode dev double-invocation consuming the single-use token twice on mount.

ui_bugs.issue: Page briefly renders the error state ('No se pudo verificar — Token inválido o ya usado') for ~200ms before the success-state + redirect to /dashboard. Cause is React 18 StrictMode dev double-invoking the verify useEffect, so the second call hits the already-consumed single-use token and overwrites local state with an error before the redirect (initiated by the first call) completes. Functionally harmless in production builds (StrictMode does NOT double-run effects in prod), but the dev/preview environment shows it. Fix: guard the verify call with a `useRef` flag (e.g. `const didRun = useRef(false); if (didRun.current) return; didRun.current = true;`) so the effect is idempotent under StrictMode.

action_items: (Optional/Low) Guard the verify-email useEffect in /app/frontend/src/pages/VerifyEmail.jsx with a useRef sentinel so React StrictMode's double-invoke does not consume the single-use token twice and briefly flash the error state before the successful redirect.
critical_code_review_comments: EmailAuthDialog.jsx mixes three modes ('login' | 'register' | 'forgot') in a single component — readable at 203 LOC but could be cleaner with a small useReducer + per-mode subcomponents if it grows. Not urgent.
VerifyEmail.jsx makes the verify call from a useEffect without a StrictMode-safe guard — see the LOW priority ui_bug above.

server.py still ~2200+ lines (carried over from iter12 review) — extracting auth routes into /app/backend/routers/auth.py would isolate the iter17 endpoints (register/verify-email/login/forgot/reset) from the existing 2000+ lines of P2P logic.

Backend uses bcrypt + httpOnly session_token cookies + brute-force lockout (verified via pytest test_brute_force_locks_out_after_5_fails). Verify-email and password-reset tokens are single-use and the test suite proves they are deleted on consumption — good security posture.
Forgot-password endpoint correctly returns the same neutral 200 for unknown users (test_forgot_password_silent_for_unknown), preventing user enumeration.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 19/19 E2E checkpoints passed across two phases (landing CTAs, dialog open, google-btn-in-dialog, toggle register, register no-autologin, register success state, forgot-mode UI, forgot submit, unverified register, unverified login msg, unverified login no cookie, verify page render, verify redirect to dashboard, verify session cookie set, reset page render, reset submit redirect, login with new password, login new pwd cookie set, toggle cycle regression)

test_credentials: Used fresh emails e2e.iter17.<ts>@example.com and e2e.iter17.unver.<ts>@example.com with password veryStrongPass123 (later changed to newStrongPwd456 for the reset test). Verification + reset tokens read directly from MongoDB users collection via /tmp/get_token.py (MONGO_URL + DB_NAME=test_database). No real email delivery exercised — Resend is best-effort and tokens are persisted in the DB.
seed_data_creation: Two fresh email-auth users created in MongoDB during E2E (one verified+pwd-reset, one left unverified). They do not collide with the pytest cleanup target cuba.iter16@example.com.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter17 Email/Password fallback is FEATURE-COMPLETE and stable end-to-end. Backend pytest target is /app/backend/tests/test_iter16_email_auth.py (run in ~13s, 17/17 pass). Do NOT run the full backend tests/ folder — known >240s timeout flagged by previous agents. To query tokens for E2E, use the helper at /tmp/get_token.py <email> <verify|reset> (reads MONGO_URL directly). Selectors confirmed working: hero-email-btn, hero-start-btn (Comenzar con Google), email-auth-dialog, auth-google-btn, auth-name-input, auth-email-input, auth-password-input, auth-submit, auth-toggle-mode, auth-forgot-link, auth-success-state, verify-email-page, reset-password-page, reset-pwd-input, reset-confirm-input, reset-submit. The only remaining nit is the LOW-priority StrictMode-double-call flash on VerifyEmail.jsx — see action_items.
rca of the issue: No production-impacting bug found. The minor UX flash on /auth/verify-email/<token> is caused by React 18 StrictMode double-invoking the verify useEffect during dev/preview renders. First call: POST verify -> 200, sets httpOnly session_token, schedules navigate('/dashboard'). Second call (StrictMode replay): POST verify with the same now-consumed single-use token -> 400, setState('error'), briefly painting 'No se pudo verificar — Token inválido o ya usado'. Then the navigate from the first call resolves and we redirect to /dashboard. Mitigation: add a useRef sentinel inside VerifyEmail.jsx's useEffect to make the call idempotent. Production builds (StrictMode does not double-run effects in prod) won't exhibit this, so it's optional.
